stimulus.content: The New England colonies had thin, rocky soil and long, cold winters. Because farming was hard, many colonists turned to the sea. They caught fish and built ships to earn a living.

stem: What did New England colonists do because their soil was rocky?

choices.text: They grew tobacco on large plantations
They turned to fishing and shipbuilding
They moved south to find better land
They traded fur with European countries

answer_explanation: Because the rocky soil and cold climate made farming difficult, New England colonists relied on the ocean for their economy, turning to fishing and shipbuilding. Tobacco plantations were a Southern colony activity.
